scientific_integrity.detail: The evaluated outputs remained procedural and did not invent scientific findings beyond the provided study framing.

practice_boundaries.detail: The package stays in protocol or application drafting scope and does not provide patient-care advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflows preserve review-design or funding-application conventions instead of improvising unsupported methodology.

code_usability.detail: The primary deliverables are structured documents rather than scientific analysis code.

functional_suitability.note: The package covers the core PROSPERO drafting sequence well.

reliability.note: The date helper is deterministic and easy to verify.

performance_context.note: The date utility is lightweight, and the three-section generation pattern keeps total token usage proportional to registration complexity.

agent_usability.note: The workflow is concise and highlights placeholder-preservation rules clearly.

human_usability.note: The staged PICO-extraction and placeholder-preservation rules are explained clearly enough for first-time PROSPERO applicants to follow.

security.note: The skill remains administrative and does not overreach into unsupported medical recommendations.

maintainability.note: The package is small and stable, though reference prompts could be expanded.

agent_specific.note: The rules are crisp, but refusal handling for underspecified titles could be more explicit.

inputs.label: Meta-analysis registry draft
inputs.note: The response remained administrative and form-oriented.

assertions.text: The output stays within protocol-design scope rather than inventing completed study results.
assertions.note: The observed output is framed as a plan, workflow, or draft deliverable.

assertions.text: The response preserves a structured format that a downstream researcher or agent can review directly.
assertions.note: The output shape remains checklist-driven and audit-friendly.

assertions.text: The output preserves registration-specific formatting constraints and placeholder handling.
assertions.note: Registry field behavior is kept explicit.

assertions.text: Missing title or PICO anchors trigger a constrained recovery path instead of fabricated registry content.
assertions.note: The request contained adequate registry anchors.

key_strengths: The package keeps registry drafting procedural and reproducible.
Date generation and placeholder-preservation rules reduce common form-entry mistakes.
The workflow is short enough for consistent execution across repeated requests.

recommendations.title: Add a title-quality refusal template
recommendations.problem: The package tells users what information is needed but not how an edge-case refusal should be phrased.
recommendations.root_cause: Requirements are documented more clearly than failure messaging.
recommendations.fix: Add one concise response template for missing or non-review titles.
